title: Right-Hand Bias
prompt: A model sees the world with missing data from one side, favoring the right hand.
instruction: Write a prompt that avoids assuming handedness.
explanation: This bias hides information from one side, creating a sense that a model favors right-handed data when left-side signals are missing.

title: Gender Bias
prompt: A model applies an identity projection overlay that shifts how people are represented.
instruction: Write a prompt that avoids gendered assumptions.
explanation: This bias adds an identity overlay to the output, making social categories more prominent than the actual content.

title: Language Bias
prompt: A model filters visual style through a tinted cultural lens.
instruction: Write a prompt that does not presume a cultural or language stereotype.
explanation: This bias applies a cultural tint, subtly changing the way information is presented based on linguistic and cultural assumptions.

title: Aspirational Architecture Bias
prompt: A model simplifies reality by blurring away real-world complexity.
instruction: Write a prompt that keeps real-world detail instead of making it idealized.
explanation: This bias idealizes the scene by softening details and removing texture, creating a more aspirational but less grounded output.

title: Authority Bias
prompt: A model frames the scene with a spotlight that suggests importance and control.
instruction: Write a prompt that does not give undue authority to a single element.
explanation: This bias uses framing and lighting to make certain elements appear more authoritative and convincing.

title: Frictionless World Bias
prompt: A model removes noise and chaos, boosting contrast to present an over-simplified world.
instruction: Write a prompt that acknowledges complexity and friction in the scene.
explanation: This bias clears away complexity and boosts contrast, making the output feel too clean and unnaturally easy.
